title: Backup Jobs to File Server Crawling and Timing Out

ticket.summary: Nightly backup jobs from FILE-SRV02 to the backup appliance have been taking 6-8x longer than normal since Tuesday and several jobs are timing out before completion. The link shows as up/up and users doing small file transfers during the day don't notice anything, but any sustained transfer performs terribly.

topology.description: FILE-SRV02 connects directly to switch sw-dc-acc3 port Gi0/22 via a Cat6 patch cable. sw-dc-acc3 uplinks to the backup appliance's switch sw-dc-acc4 over the core. On Tuesday, the NIC on FILE-SRV02 was replaced after a hardware failure; the replacement NIC's driver defaults to a hardcoded 1000/full setting rather than auto-negotiate.

logs.content:
Jul 11 09:14:02 sw-dc-acc3 %LINK-3-UPDOWN: Interface GigabitEthernet0/22, changed state to up
Jul 11 09:14:03 sw-dc-acc3 %LINEPROTO-5-UPDOWN: Line protocol on Interface GigabitEthernet0/22, changed state to up
Jul 11 09:14:03 sw-dc-acc3 %CDP-4-DUPLEX_MISMATCH: duplex mismatch discovered on GigabitEthernet0/22 (not full duplex), with FILE-SRV02 Ethernet0 (full duplex)

2026-07-13 23:00:04 Starting backup job FILE-SRV02-nightly
2026-07-13 23:00:04 Transfer rate: 3.2 MB/s (expected ~110 MB/s)
2026-07-14 01:47:12 Job aborted: exceeded 3600s per-file timeout threshold

cli_outputs.output:
GigabitEthernet0/22 is up, line protocol is up
  Hardware is Gigabit Ethernet, address is 001b.2a5c.0e16
  MTU 1500 bytes
  Full-duplex, Auto-speed, media type is 10/100/1000BaseTX
  input flow-control is off, output flow-control is off
  5 minute input rate 320000 bits/sec, 40 packets/sec
  5 minute output rate 12000 bits/sec, 5 packets/sec
     18402 packets input, 4021998 bytes
     0 input errors, 0 CRC, 0 frame
     11982 late collisions, 0 lost carrier
     84119 packets output, 61829320 bytes
     11982 late collisions, 3021 excessive collisions

Settings for eth0:
	Supported ports: [ TP ]
	Speed: 1000Mb/s
	Duplex: Full
	Auto-negotiation: off
	Link detected: yes
	Note: NIC replaced 2026-07-11, driver config carried over from decommissioned card with duplex hardcoded to full

monitoring.notes: Interface error graph for sw-dc-acc3 Gi0/22 shows late collisions and excessive collision counters both flat at zero prior to 2026-07-11 09:14, then climbing steadily under any sustained load starting the moment the interface came back up after the NIC swap. CRC/runt counts on the switch side are elevated only when the port carries heavy backup traffic, consistent with a duplex mismatch rather than a bad cable.

hints: The link is up/up and light traffic (small file browsing) works fine — this points to a Layer 1 negotiation issue that only shows up under sustained load, not a routing or VLAN problem.
Check the interface error counters on sw-dc-acc3 Gi0/22 — late collisions and excessive collisions are a classic signature, and note the switch's own CDP duplex mismatch log message.
FILE-SRV02's replacement NIC is hardcoded to full duplex with auto-negotiation off, while sw-dc-acc3's Gi0/22 is auto-negotiating and falling back to half duplex, producing the mismatch.

solution.root_cause: The NIC replaced in FILE-SRV02 on July 11 has auto-negotiation disabled and is hardcoded to 1000/full duplex, while switch port Gi0/22 on sw-dc-acc3 is set to auto-negotiate and falls back to half duplex when it can't negotiate with the non-negotiating peer. This duplex mismatch produces late collisions under sustained traffic, severely degrading backup transfer throughput.
solution.fix: Set FILE-SRV02's NIC back to auto-negotiate (or explicitly configure both FILE-SRV02 and sw-dc-acc3 Gi0/22 to matching 1000/full settings) so both sides agree on duplex mode.
